interactions.summary: Jiang completes the Zhu Yuanzhang story: the emperor kills the best scorers because a fair system is politically useless if it lets localities rebel against the center.

speaker_notes.note: Closing lecture monologue; no independent student response in the focus segment.

claims.claim: The Keju's real purpose is not fairness or transparency but preventing localities from rebelling against the center.
claims.temporal_scope: Ming Keju story used as general explanation of the exam system

signature_moments.moment: The emperor wants not a fair exam but a geography of obedience.
signature_moments.source_phrase: he wants a system in which the localities will not rebel against the center
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This is the lecture's final compression of the Keju: fairness is subordinate to center-periphery control.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: The imperial priority Jiang assigns to the Keju: keeping provinces and local elites from becoming coordinated challengers to the center.

chronology_notes.note: The lecture closes by pointing to Genghis Khan as the next class topic, placing this episode immediately before the Mongol follow-up.
